 {"id":"bd-ktng","title":"Optimize CLI test suite - eliminate redundant git init calls","description":"Current: Each of 13 CLI tests calls git init (31s total). Solution: Use single test binary built once in init(), skip git operations where possible, or use mock filesystem.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.660276-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.660276-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ktng","depends_on_id":"bd-l5gq","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.662102-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ky74","title":"Optimize cmd/bd long-mode tests by switching to in-process testing","description":"The long-mode CLI tests in cmd/bd are slow (1.4-4.4 seconds each) because they spawn full bd processes via exec.Command() for every operation.

Current approach:
- Each runBD() call spawns new bd process via exec.Command(testBD, args...)
- Each process initializes Go runtime, loads SQLite, parses CLI flags
- Tests run serially (create → update → show → close)
- Even with --no-daemon flag, there's significant process spawn overhead

Example timing from test run:
- TestCLI_PriorityFormats: 2.21s
- TestCLI_Show: 2.26s
- TestCLI_Ready: 2.29s
- TestCLI_Import: 4.42s

Optimization strategy:
1. Convert most tests to in-process testing (call bd functions directly)
2. Reuse test databases across related operations instead of fresh init each time
3. Keep a few exec.Command() tests that batch multiple operations to verify the CLI path works end-to-end

This should reduce test time from ~40s to ~5s for the affected tests.","notes":"Converted all CLI tests in cli_fast_test.go to use in-process testing via rootCmd.Execute(). Created runBDInProcess() helper that:
- Calls rootCmd.Execute() directly instead of spawning processes
- Uses mutex to serialize execution (rootCmd/viper not thread-safe)
- Properly cleans up global state (store, daemonClient)
- Returns only stdout to avoid JSON parsing issues with stderr warnings

Performance results:
- In-process tests: ~0.6s each (cached even faster)
- exec.Command tests: ~3.7s each  
- Speedup: ~10x faster

Kept TestCLI_EndToEnd() that uses exec.Command for end-to-end validation of the actual binary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-08T18:40:27.358821-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T18:47:11.107998-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T18:47:11.107998-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-kzxd","title":"Sync protection mechanism resurrects deleted issues","description":"During bd sync, the 'Protecting N issue(s) from left snapshot' logic resurrects issues that are in the deletions manifest, causing UNIQUE constraint failures on subsequent syncs.

## Reproduction
1. Delete an issue with bd delete (creates tombstone + deletions.jsonl entry)
2. Run bd sync - succeeds
3. Run bd sync again - fails with 'UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id'

## Root Cause
The protection mechanism (designed to prevent data loss during 3-way merge) keeps a snapshot of issues before sync. When importing after pull, it restores issues from this snapshot even if they're in the deletions manifest.

## Observed Behavior
- bd-3pd was deleted and added to deletions.jsonl
- First sync exports tombstone, commits, pulls, imports - succeeds
- Second sync: protection restores bd-3pd from left snapshot
- Import tries to create bd-3pd which already exists → UNIQUE constraint error

## Expected Behavior
Issues in deletions manifest should NOT be restored by protection mechanism.

## Workaround
Manually delete from DB: sqlite3 .beads/beads.db 'DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = "bd-xxx"'

## Files to Investigate
- cmd/bd/sync.go - protection logic
- cmd/bd/snapshot_manager.go - left snapshot handling
- internal/importer/importer.go - import with protection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T10:11:02.550663-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:20:51.651662-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T10:20:51.651662-08:00"}

Should warn not fail.  {"id":"bd-l4b6","title":"Add tests for bd init --team wizard","description":"Write integration tests for the team wizard:
- Test branch detection
- Test sync branch creation
- Test protected branch workflow
- Test auto-sync configuration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-05T18:58:18.192425-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-06T20:06:49.22056-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-06T19:55:39.687439-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-l5gq","title":"Optimize test suite performance - cut runtime by 50%+","description":"## Problem
Test suite takes ~20.8 seconds, with 95% of time spent in just 2 tests:
- TestHashIDs_MultiCloneConverge: 11.08s (53%)
- TestHashIDs_IdenticalContentDedup: 8.78s (42%)

Both tests in beads_hash_multiclone_test.go perform extensive Git operations (bare repos, multiple clones, sync rounds).

## Goal
Cut total test time by at least 50% (to ~10 seconds or less).

## Analysis
Tests already have some optimizations:
- --shared --depth=1 --no-tags for fast cloning
- Disabled hooks, gc, fsync
- Support -short flag

## Impact
- Faster development feedback loop
- Reduced CI costs and time
- Better developer experience","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-04T01:23:14.410648-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.683213-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.683213-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-l7u","title":"Duplicate DefaultRetentionDays constants","description":"## Problem

There are now two constants for the same value:

1. `deletions.DefaultRetentionDays = 7` in `internal/deletions/deletions.go:184`
2. `configfile.DefaultDeletionsRetentionDays = 7` in `internal/configfile/configfile.go:102`

## Impact
- DRY violation
- Risk of values getting out of sync
- Confusing which one to use

## Fix
Remove the constant from `deletions` package and have it import from `configfile`, or create a shared constants package.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:49:38.356211-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:15:21.964842-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:15:21.964842-08:00"}
